title: Julbernardia gossweileri
uses: Julbernardia gossweileri is used in traditional medicine, particularly in West Africa, where it is valued for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant's leaves and bark are used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. In traditional medicine, Julbernardia gossweileri is also used to treat gastrointestinal issues and as a remedy for snake bites. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to the presence of flavonoids, alkaloids, and other bioactive compounds. Further research is needed to confirm the full extent of Julbernardia gossweileri's medicinal uses and potential applications.
taxonomy: Julbernardia gossweileri is classified as a species of tree in the family Meliaceae. This family is commonly known as the mahogany family. It is a genus of trees that are native to tropical Africa. Julbernardia gossweileri is further classified as a member of the order Sapindales and the kingdom Plantae. The exact classification of this species may vary depending on the source, but it is generally considered to be a type of tropical tree.

The plant's extracts have been found to exhibit antibacterial and antifungal activities, making it useful in treating various infections.
